title: Golden Boot remains elusive for Messi: Can he still win it in World Cup 2026 or lose to Mbappe’s historic tally?
content:
• Kylian Mbappe currently leads the 2026 World Cup Golden Boot race with 10 goals and 4 assists for France.
• Kylian Mbappe has become football's most prolific World Cup scorer with 22 career goals in 22 appearances, surpassing Lionel Messi's previous record of 21 career goals in 31 matches.
• Mbappe is the first player with 10+ goals in one World Cup tournament since West Germany's Gerd Muller achieved it in 1970.

title: Can saying this abusive word lead to an obscenity case? Supreme Court explains in Tamil Nadu land dispute case
content:
• The Supreme Court clarified that the use of abusive or vulgar language does not by itself amount to criminal obscenity under Section 294(b) of the Indian Penal Code, requiring words to be lascivious, appeal to prurient interests, and tend to deprave or corrupt.
• The Supreme Court upheld a conviction for causing grievous hurt under Section 326 of the IPC, reducing the sentence to imprisonment till the rising of the court and ordering a fine of ₹50,000.
• The ruling, delivered by a Bench comprising Justices Sanjay Karol and Vipul M Pancholi, stemmed from an agricultural land dispute in Tamil Nadu that occurred in August 2017.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Final: How much will Shakira, Madonna, BTS and Justin Bieber earn for halftime show?
content:
• The FIFA World Cup 2026 final will be the first ever to include a Super Bowl-style musical halftime spectacle.
• Artists performing at the FIFA World Cup 2026 final halftime show, including Shakira, Madonna, BTS, and Justin Bieber, will not receive a fixed performance fee; instead, organizers will cover production, staging, and logistics costs.
• The FIFA World Cup 2026 final, featuring a star-studded halftime show, is expected to be played between Spain and Argentina.

title: Delhi restaurants, cafes to stay open till 4 am for Argentina vs Spain FIFA World Cup final: 'One night that...'
content:
• Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta announced that restaurants and cafes in Delhi can remain open till 4 AM for the Argentina vs Spain FIFA World Cup final, under the Delhi government's 24x7 business-friendly operating framework.
• The Karnataka government allowed hotels and restaurants in Bengaluru to extend food service timings until 3:30 AM on July 14, 15, and 19 for FIFA World Cup matches, following a request from the National Restaurant Association of India (NRAI).
• Bengaluru Police Commissioner Seemanth Kumar Singh issued an order under the Karnataka Police Act, permitting late-night screening of matches and extended food service until 3:30 AM in Bengaluru.

title: Gujarat tops NITI Aayog's Investment Friendliness Index 2026
content:
• Gujarat secured the top position in NITI Aayog's first-ever Investment Friendliness Index 2026, achieving the highest score of 56.6, ahead of Maharashtra (53.7) and Tamil Nadu (53.3) among 17 major States.
• In FY 2024, Gujarat recorded the lowest fiscal deficit among all States at 2.81% of GSDP, and its outstanding liabilities stood at around 18% of GSDP.
• Gujarat's per capita GSDP is ₹2,64,232, which is nearly 67% higher than the average of major States, and the State contributes 31% of India's total merchandise exports.

title: States' fiscal deficit likely to moderate to 3.4% of GSDP in FY27 as tax collections improve: ICICI Bank
content:
• States' fiscal deficit is expected to moderate to 3.4 per cent of GSDP in FY27, according to an ICICI Bank report.
• The fiscal deficit for states had widened to 3.6 per cent of GSDP in FY26 due to muted tax collections and higher expenditure.
• For a sample of 24 states, own tax revenue grew 16.4 per cent year-on-year during April-May 2026, supported by a 22 per cent rise in SGST and an 18 per cent increase in stamp duty collections.

title: HARMAN collaborates with city university on automobile tech
content:
• Nearly 80% of HARMAN's 4,000 automotive engineers in India are based in Bengaluru, working on next-generation automotive software.
• Krishna Kumar holds the positions of Senior Vice President, Managing Director, and Automotive Head for HARMAN India.
• HARMAN serves as the sole Tier 1 supplier for cockpit and infotainment systems for Tata Motors, including its electric vehicles, with all development occurring in Bengaluru.

title: Inaugural ILCA Open India International Sailing Championship concludes in Hyderabad
content:
• The Inaugural ILCA Open India International Sailing Championship concluded on Saturday, July 18, in Hyderabad.
• The championship was held at the EME Sailing Association (EMESA) on Hussainsagar Lake and Lieutenant General Neeraj Varshney, Commandant of MCEME, presided over the closing ceremony.
• The event featured 124 sailors, including 19 women and girls, representing 19 sailing clubs and institutions, along with international participants.

title: Rice federation tells T.N. Chief Minister to take up waiver for rice with GST Council
content:
• Rice packs weighing below 26 kg are currently taxed at 5% GST, which millers state affects daily wage labourers buying in small quantities.
• The 57th GST Council is likely to be convened in a few weeks, where States can raise their concerns regarding various issues, including the GST waiver for rice.
• Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay has been urged by paddy and rice millers to take up the issue of Goods and Services Tax (GST) waiver for rice with the Centre and the GST Council.

title: 30 new traffic patrol bikes inducted to strengthen Hyderabad traffic management
content:
• On Saturday, July 18, 30 new Avenger patrol bikes, procured with CSR contributions worth ₹1.6 crore, were inducted into Hyderabad's traffic management, increasing the city's operational traffic patrol bike fleet to 80 from 50.
• The Hyderabad City Security Council (HCSC) has deployed 120 Traffic Marshals over the past six months to assist the traffic police.
• The 'Spandana' initiative, an all-women safety patrol with 10 operational vehicles, was launched two months ago in Hyderabad to provide immediate assistance to women and girls in distress.
